dev mode. examine repo to onboard

then evaluate this spec:

Spec: Eval-Evidence Provenance for Composable Beliefs

  Purpose: Let CB hold the evidence produced by a published eval, so that every verdict traces back to the exact model run that
  produced it. This is the only new capability required for the first AmIEval eval. Everything else reuses existing CB.

  Context for the implementer

  CB already has: primitive / compound / implication assertions; typed claims with a source/artifact URI scheme (document:, web:,
  user:, session:, etc.); immutability + supersession + staleness cascade; dependency tracking (deps.json); the mix bs query shell;
  and compile-to-NL (used in production to generate CLAUDE.md from the graph).

  Do not redesign any of that. This spec adds one source scheme and a handful of queries on top of the existing schema
  (lib/cb/assertion.ex is the SSOT). If a requirement here seems to need a new subsystem, stop and flag it — it almost certainly
  doesn't.

  The one capability being added

  A new eval: source scheme for primitive assertions, carrying structured provenance so an atomic observation is bound to a specific
  (eval, run, case, model, ruler) tuple.

  Data model: the eval: primitive

  A primitive grounded in an eval run carries an eval: artifact URI plus this structured provenance payload (extend the existing
  primitive's source/evidence fields; do not create a parallel record type):

  ┌───────────────┬─────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │     Field     │        Type         │                                        Meaning                                         │
  ├───────────────┼─────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ eval_id       │ string              │ Stable id for the eval study (e.g. silent-data-loss-v1)                                │
  ├───────────────┼─────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ run_id        │ string              │ One execution of the harness over the dataset                                          │
  ├───────────────┼─────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ case_id       │ string              │ The test case / sample within the dataset                                              │
  ├───────────────┼─────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ model         │ string              │ Model under test (e.g. claude-opus-4-8)                                                │
  ├───────────────┼─────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ model_version │ string              │ Snapshot/version/date — the key supersession pivots on                                 │
  ├───────────────┼─────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ ruler         │ string              │ Which scorer produced this observation (e.g. deterministic-fielddiff,                  │
  │               │                     │ llm-judge-vanilla, cb-judge)                                                           │
  ├───────────────┼─────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ outcome       │ enum + optional     │ The scored result (e.g. silent_loss | flagged | correct, plus optional numeric)        │
  │               │ value               │                                                                                        │
  ├───────────────┼─────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ harness       │ string              │ e.g. inspect                                                                           │
  ├───────────────┼─────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ artifact_ref  │ string (path/URI)   │ Pointer to the raw logged output, for audit                                            │
  └───────────────┴─────────────────────┴────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  The primitive's claim text is the human-readable statement of that one observation (e.g. "On case 7, run 3, claude-opus-4-8
  silently omitted record #7; deterministic ruler scored silent_loss.").

  Granularity rule

  One primitive per (run_id, case_id, ruler) observation. This keeps each primitive atomic (CB's existing "one claim per primitive"
  rule) and lets a compound compose across rulers to express agreement — which is how earned confidence is recorded. Do not pack
  multiple rulers' verdicts into one primitive.

  What reuses existing CB unchanged

  - Compounds = inferences over a set of eval: primitives (e.g. "silent-loss rate = 11% across 50 cases for model X", or "all three
  rulers agree case 7 is a silent loss"). Uses existing compound + deps mechanism. No new code.
  - Implications = the verdict/recommendation (e.g. "do not trust class-Y agents with bulk record ops"). Existing mechanism.
  - Supersession / staleness = existing cascade. The new requirement is only that model_version is a first-class, queryable field so
  that "a newer model_version arrived" can flag dependent compounds/implications stale.

  Operations to add (queries on mix bs)

  Minimal additions to the existing shell:

  - bs list --eval <eval_id> — all assertions for an eval
  - bs list --model <model> [--version <v>] — observations by model/version
  - filter support for --case <case_id>, --ruler <ruler>, --outcome <outcome>
  - bs stale --model-version <model> <old_v> — flag verdicts resting on a superseded model version (compose with existing
  stale/--cascade)

  bs tree <implication_id> must already render verdict → compounds → primitives; verify it surfaces each primitive's artifact_ref so
  the chain is auditable end to end.

  Compile-to-NL (extend existing)

  Add an eval-writeup target: given a root implication, render markdown showing the verdict, its supporting compounds, and the
  underlying primitives with their artifact_refs. If a compile-to-NL pipeline exists, add a template; if not, a thin graph-traversal
  + template is sufficient. The graph stays the single source of truth — the writeup is a generated view, never hand-edited.

  Acceptance criteria

  1. Can create an eval: primitive with all provenance fields; invalid/missing required fields are rejected (validation lives with
  the schema in lib/cb/assertion.ex).
  2. Can query primitives by eval_id, model(+version), case_id, ruler, and outcome.
  3. A compound can depend on a set of eval: primitives, and bs tree on the resulting implication shows the full chain down to each
  primitive's artifact_ref.
  4. Introducing primitives with a newer model_version flags dependent compounds/implications stale via the existing cascade.
  5. Compiling a root implication emits a markdown writeup containing the verdict, supporting compounds, and primitives with
  artifact_refs.

  Non-goals (do NOT build)

  - No Bittensor / stake-weighted consensus subnet.
  - No numeric-confidence aggregation engine. (Earned confidence is expressed structurally, via cross-ruler agreement compounds — not
  a computed score.)
  - No new storage backend, no web UI, no service/hosting/monetization features.
  - No schema redesign or new record type — extend the existing primitive.
  - Harness integration is out of the minimum. Getting Inspect logs into CB can start as a manual step or a throwaway script. A clean
  inspect → eval: primitives importer is a later, optional adapter, not part of this spec. Flag it as a follow-up; don't build it
  now.

  Worked example (the first eval, in miniature)

  primitive p1  eval:silent-data-loss-v1/run3  case=7 model=claude-opus-4-8 ruler=deterministic outcome=silent_loss
  artifact_ref=logs/run3/case7.json
  primitive p2  eval:silent-data-loss-v1/run3  case=7 model=claude-opus-4-8 ruler=llm-judge   outcome=silent_loss
  artifact_ref=logs/run3/case7.json
  compound  c1  "all rulers agree: case 7 is a silent loss"   deps=[p1, p2]
  implication i1 "claude-opus-4-8 silently drops records on >10-item lists; do not use unguarded for bulk record ops"  deps=[c1, ...]

  bs tree i1 walks i1 → c1 → p1/p2 → the raw artifact_refs. That traversal is the auditable-credibility property the whole practice
  rests on.

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

addedBlocks:
## claude-in-chrome
**IMPORTANT: Before using any chrome browser tools, you MUST first load them using ToolSearch.**

Chrome browser tools are MCP tools that require loading before use. Before calling any mcp__claude-in-chrome__* tool:
1. Use ToolSearch with `select:mcp__claude-in-chrome__<tool_name>` to load the specific tool
2. Then call the tool

For example, to get tab context:
1. First: ToolSearch with query "select:mcp__claude-in-chrome__tabs_context_mcp"
2. Then: Call mcp__claude-in-chrome__tabs_context_mcp

## context7
Use this server to fetch current documentation whenever the user asks about a library, framework, SDK, API, CLI tool, or cloud service -- even well-known ones like React, Next.js, Prisma, Express, Tailwind, Django, or Spring Boot. This includes API syntax, configuration, version migration, library-specific debugging, setup instructions, and CLI tool usage. Use even when you think you know the answer -- your training data may not reflect recent changes. Prefer this over web search for library docs.

Do not use for: refactoring, writing scripts from scratch, debugging business logic, code review, or general programming concepts.

attachment.content:
- merge-worktrees: Clean up orphaned worktree branches that weren't merged by /end (crashed sessions, etc). Use when the user says "merge worktrees", "clean up worktrees", or invokes "/merge-worktrees".
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

173	      name: map["name"],

defmodule CB.Belief do
  @moduledoc """
  Belief struct and JSON serialization.

  Beliefs are the nodes of the composable beliefs DAG - structured claims
  about the world that the agent can query, compose, and act on. Stored
  in the belief graph JSON file (see `CB.Config.beliefs_path/0`).

  ## Self-referential schema

  The DAG's own schema can be expressed as contracts in the DAG. Those
  contracts are the source of truth - this moduledoc points, it does
  not restate. Use `mix cb.verify.schema` to check this module against
  the contracts mechanically.

  By convention the schema discipline is split across a small family of
  contracts:

  | Contract | Governs |
  |---|---|
  | master schema | artifact field, contract field, enum-validated kind/domain/artifact-scheme |
  | kind enum | canonical kind values |
  | artifact-scheme enum | canonical artifact-scheme values |
  | domain enum | canonical domain values |
  | status lifecycle | status lifecycle and immutability |

  The `confidence`, `source`, and `implication` fields were expunged from
  earlier revisions of the schema: confidence as a subjective metric doing
  no load-bearing work, `source` renamed to the structured `:artifact`
  field, and `:implication` deleted in favor of a structural `:contract`
  boolean marking contract-grade implications. Use `CB.Belief.support/1`
  for structural-support metrics derived from the belief's own graph
  position.

  ## Struct summary

  Three structural types (`primitive`, `compound`, `implication`) are the
  only values of the `type` field. Contract-grade implications carry
  `contract: true` and non-empty `rules` and/or `invariants`; detect them
  via `CB.Belief.contract?/1`. `kind`, `domain`, and the artifact's scheme
  are enum-validated. `tags` is a flat list of strings for cross-cutting
  concerns.
  """

  @types ~w(primitive compound implication)
  @statuses ~w(active superseded retracted retired)

  @fields [
    :id,
    :type,
    :kind,
    :domain,
    :tags,
    :name,
    :who,
    :claim,
    :rules,
    :invariants,
    :contract,
    :artifact,
    :evidence,
    :subjects,
    :deps,
    :materialized,
    :status,
    :superseded_by,
    :retracted_on,
    :retracted_reason,
    :created,
    :_keys
  ]

  @ordered_keys ~w(id type kind domain tags name who claim rules invariants contract artifact evidence subjects deps materialized status superseded_by retracted_on retracted_reason created)

  # Canonical inner-key orders for nested objects. Matches existing data
  # convention so round-trips are byte-stable. Extra keys (not listed)
  # are appended in their original insertion order.
  @evidence_key_order ~w(date detail artifact proposed sent section)
  @subject_key_order ~w(ref type)

  defstruct @fields

  @type t :: %__MODULE__{
          id: String.t() | nil,
          type: String.t(),
          kind: String.t() | nil,
          domain: String.t() | nil,
          tags: list(String.t()),
          name: String.t() | nil,
          who: String.t() | nil,
          claim: String.t() | nil,
          rules: list(map()),
          invariants: list(String.t()),
          contract: boolean() | nil,
          artifact: String.t() | nil,
          evidence: list(map()),
          subjects: list(map()),
          deps: list(String.t()),
          materialized: map() | nil,
          status: String.t() | nil,
          superseded_by: String.t() | nil,
          retracted_on: String.t() | nil,
          retracted_reason: String.t() | nil,
          created: String.t() | nil,
          _keys: MapSet.t() | nil
        }

  @doc """
  Check if a belief is contract-grade.

  Reads the structural `:contract` field. The biconditional with non-empty
  `rules`/`invariants` is enforced by `mix cb.verify.schema`; this predicate
  trusts the field.
  """
  def contract?(%__MODULE__{contract: true}), do: true
  def contract?(_), do: false

  @doc "Valid structural types."
  def types, do: @types

  @doc "Valid status values."
  def statuses, do: @statuses

  @doc """
  Structural-support metrics derived from the belief's own graph
  position.

  Replaces the retired `confidence` field. Returns a map of
  deterministic counts - no subjective scoring. Callers that need a
  ranking signal should aggregate or weight these structural facts;
  there is no single scalar.

  Fields:
  - `:artifact_count` - distinct artifacts across the belief's own
    `artifact` field + its evidence entries' `artifact` fields
  - `:evidence_count` - count of evidence entries
  - `:dep_count` - count of upstream dependencies

  Inbound reference count (how many beliefs cite this one) requires
  graph traversal and is exposed separately via `CB.Belief.Graph`.
  """
  @spec support(t()) :: %{
          artifact_count: non_neg_integer(),
          evidence_count: non_neg_integer(),
          dep_count: non_neg_integer()
        }
  def support(%__MODULE__{} = b) do
    evidence_artifacts =
      (b.evidence || [])
      |> Enum.map(fn e -> e["artifact"] end)
      |> Enum.reject(&is_nil/1)

    artifacts =
      [b.artifact | evidence_artifacts]
      |> Enum.reject(&is_nil/1)
      |> Enum.uniq()

    %{
      artifact_count: length(artifacts),
      evidence_count: length(b.evidence || []),
      dep_count: length(b.deps || [])
    }
  end

  @doc "Convert a JSON map (string keys) to a Belief struct."
  def from_map(map) when is_map(map) do
    %__MODULE__{
      id: map["id"],
      type: map["type"],
      kind: map["kind"],
      domain: map["domain"],
      tags: map["tags"] || [],
      name: map["name"],
      who: map["who"],
      claim: map["claim"],
      rules: map["rules"] || [],
      invariants: map["invariants"] || [],
      contract: map["contract"],
      artifact: map["artifact"],
      evidence: map["evidence"] || [],
      subjects: map["subjects"] || [],
      deps: map["deps"] || [],
      materialized: map["materialized"],
      status: map["status"],
      superseded_by: map["superseded_by"],
      retracted_on: map["retracted_on"],
      retracted_reason: map["retracted_reason"],
      created: map["created"],
      _keys:
        MapSet.new(Map.keys(map))
        |> MapSet.delete("confidence")
        |> MapSet.delete("source")
        |> MapSet.delete("implication")
    }
  end

  @doc "Convert a Belief struct to a `Jason.OrderedObject` for serialization."
  def to_map(%__MODULE__{} = a) do
    present_keys = a._keys || MapSet.new(@ordered_keys)

    pairs =
      for key <- @ordered_keys, MapSet.member?(present_keys, key) do
        atom = String.to_existing_atom(key)
        value = Map.get(a, atom)
        {key, order_nested(key, value)}
      end

    Jason.OrderedObject.new(pairs)
  end

  # Wrap nested evidence/subject items so their inner keys serialize in
  # canonical order, preventing churn on round-trips.
  defp order_nested("evidence", items) when is_list(items) do
    Enum.map(items, &order_map(&1, @evidence_key_order))
  end

  defp order_nested("subjects", items) when is_list(items) do
    Enum.map(items, &order_map(&1, @subject_key_order))
  end

  defp order_nested(_key, value), do: value

  defp order_map(map, key_order) when is_map(map) and not is_struct(map) do
    known =
      for key <- key_order, Map.has_key?(map, key) do
        {key, Map.get(map, key)}
      end

    # Preserve any extra keys not in the canonical order at the end,
    # sorted for determinism.
    extra =
      map
      |> Map.drop(key_order)
      |> Enum.sort_by(fn {k, _} -> k end)

    Jason.OrderedObject.new(known ++ extra)
  end

  defp order_map(value, _), do: value
end

1	defmodule Mix.Tasks.Bs do
2	  @moduledoc """
3	  Belief shell - deterministic query interface to the composable beliefs DAG.
4	
5	  Pure graph traversal - no LLM reasoning, same input always produces
6	  the same output.
7	
8	      list [filters]       List beliefs matching filters
9	      show <id>            Full detail on a single belief
10	      tree <id>            Dependency tree visualization
11	      deps <id>            Direct dependencies (--deep for full tree)
12	      dependents <id>      Reverse dependency lookup (--deep for transitive)
13	      stale                Find beliefs with superseded/retracted deps (--cascade)
14	      path <id1> <id2>     Find connection between two beliefs
15	      history <id>         Supersession chain
16	      subjects <ref|type>  Find beliefs by subject
17	      stats                Graph-level statistics
18	
19	  ## Ids
20	
21	  Ids may be given bare (`c029`) or namespaced (`cb:c029`). A bare local id
22	  resolves to its namespaced form when exactly one belief matches; if more
23	  than one namespace carries that local id, qualify it explicitly. `mix bs
24	  <id>` with no verb is shorthand for `show`.
25	
26	  ## Collections
27	
28	  `--beliefs PATH` points the shell at an alternate belief graph for one
29	  command (the `CB_BELIEFS` env var does the same persistently) - used to
30	  explore other belief collections without touching the main graph.
31	
32	  ## Filters (for list)
33	
34	      primitive|compound|implication    Filter by structural type
35	      active|superseded|retracted|all   Filter by status (default: active)
36	      contracts                         Contract-grade implications only
37	      unlinked                          Implications with no materialized items
38	      tag:<tag> / --tag <tag>           Filter by tag
39	      kind:<kind>                       Filter by semantic kind
40	      domain:<domain>                   Filter by domain
41	      subject_type:<type>              Filter by subject type
42	      <namespace>/<ref>                Filter by subject ref
43	      -v / --verbose                    Show full detail
44	  """
45	  @shortdoc "Belief shell - query and traverse the composable beliefs DAG"
46	
47	  use Mix.Task
48	
49	  import CB.Display
50	
51	  alias CB.Belief.{Filter, Formatter, Graph, Store}
52	
53	  @impl Mix.Task
54	  def run(args) do
55	    args = apply_beliefs_override(args)
56	    {flags, positional} = extract_flags(args)
57	
58	    case positional do
59	      ["list" | rest] -> cmd_list(rest ++ flag_args(flags))
60	      ["show", id | _] -> cmd_show(id)
61	      ["tree", id | _] -> cmd_tree(id)
62	      ["deps", id | _] -> cmd_deps(id, flags)
63	      ["dependents", id | _] -> cmd_dependents(id, flags)
64	      ["stale" | _] -> cmd_stale(flags)
65	      ["path", id1, id2 | _] -> cmd_path(id1, id2)
66	      ["history", id | _] -> cmd_history(id)
67	      ["subjects" | rest] -> cmd_subjects(rest)
68	      ["stats" | _] -> cmd_stats()
69	      ["help" | _] -> cmd_help()
70	      [] -> cmd_help()
71	
72	      [cmd | _] ->
73	        if Regex.match?(~r/^([a-z][a-z0-9-]*:)?[ac]\d+$/, cmd) do
74	          cmd_show(cmd)
75	        else
76	          IO.puts(:stderr, "Unknown command: #{cmd}")
77	          IO.puts(:stderr, "Run `mix bs help` for usage.")
78	          System.halt(1)
79	        end
80	    end
81	  end
82	
83	  # --- Commands ---
84	
85	  defp cmd_list(args) do
86	    {filters, opts} = Filter.parse_args(args)
87	
88	    for arg <- Keyword.get_values(opts, :unknown) do
89	      IO.puts(:stderr, "Unknown filter: #{arg}")
90	      System.halt(1)
91	    end
92	
93	    {:ok, beliefs} = Store.read()
94	    total = length(beliefs)
95	
96	    filtered = beliefs |> Filter.apply_filters(filters) |> Filter.sort()
97	
98	    lines =
99	      if Keyword.get(opts, :verbose) do
100	        Enum.flat_map(filtered, &Formatter.detail/1) ++
101	          ["#{length(filtered)} beliefs (of #{total} total)"]
102	      else
103	        Formatter.table(filtered, total)
104	      end
105	
106	    Enum.each(lines, &IO.puts/1)
107	  end
108	
109	  defp cmd_show(id) do
110	    {:ok, beliefs} = Store.read()
111	    id = resolve_or_halt(beliefs, id)
112	    belief = Enum.find(beliefs, &(&1.id == id))
113	    Formatter.detail(belief) |> Enum.each(&IO.puts/1)
114	  end
115	
116	  defp cmd_tree(id) do
117	    {:ok, beliefs} = Store.read()
118	    id = resolve_or_halt(beliefs, id)
119	    belief = Enum.find(beliefs, &(&1.id == id))
120	    Formatter.tree(belief, beliefs) |> Enum.each(&IO.puts/1)
121	  end
122	
123	  defp cmd_deps(id, flags) do
124	    {:ok, beliefs} = Store.read()
125	    id = resolve_or_halt(beliefs, id)
126	    idx = Graph.index(beliefs)
127	    belief = Map.get(idx, id)
128	
129	    if Keyword.get(flags, :deep) do
130	      cmd_tree(id)
131	    else
132	      resolved = Graph.resolve_deps(belief, idx)
133	
134	      if resolved == [] do
135	        IO.puts("#{id} has no dependencies (primitive).")
136	      else
137	        Formatter.table(resolved, length(beliefs)) |> Enum.each(&IO.puts/1)
138	      end
139	    end
140	  end
141	
142	  defp cmd_dependents(id, flags) do
143	    {:ok, beliefs} = Store.read()
144	    id = resolve_or_halt(beliefs, id)
145	    deep = Keyword.get(flags, :deep, false)
146	    results = Graph.dependents(id, beliefs, deep: deep)
147	
148	    if results == [] do
149	      IO.puts("Nothing depends on #{id}.")
150	    else
151	      label = if deep, do: "deep dependents", else: "dependents"
152	      IO.puts("")
153	      IO.puts("#{length(results)} #{label} of #{id}:")
154	      IO.puts("")
155	      Formatter.table(results, length(beliefs)) |> Enum.each(&IO.puts/1)
156	    end
157	  end
158	
159	  defp cmd_stale(flags) do
160	    {:ok, beliefs} = Store.read()
161	    cascade = Keyword.get(flags, :cascade, false)
162	    results = Graph.stale(beliefs, cascade: cascade)
163	
164	    if results == [] do
165	      IO.puts("No stale beliefs found.")
166	    else
167	      idx = Graph.index(beliefs)
168	      label = if cascade, do: " (with cascade)", else: ""
169	      IO.puts("")
170	      IO.puts("Stale beliefs#{label}:")
171	      IO.puts("")
172	
173	      Enum.each(results, fn {a, bad_deps} ->
174	        IO.puts("  #{a.id} #{a.claim}")
175	
176	        Enum.each(bad_deps, fn dep_id ->
177	          dep = Map.get(idx, dep_id)
178	
179	          reason =
180	            cond do
181	              dep == nil -> "missing"
182	              dep.superseded_by -> "superseded by #{dep.superseded_by}"
183	              dep.status == "retracted" -> "retracted"
184	              true -> dep.status
185	            end
186	
187	          IO.puts("    #{dep_id}: #{reason}")
188	        end)
189	
190	        IO.puts("")
191	      end)
192	
193	      IO.puts("#{length(results)} stale belief(s)")
194	    end
195	  end
196	
197	  defp cmd_path(id1, id2) do
198	    {:ok, beliefs} = Store.read()
199	    id1 = resolve_or_halt(beliefs, id1)
200	    id2 = resolve_or_halt(beliefs, id2)
201	    idx = Graph.index(beliefs)
202	
203	    case Graph.path(id1, id2, idx, beliefs) do
204	      {:ok, path} ->
205	        IO.puts("")
206	        IO.puts("Path from #{id1} to #{id2} (#{length(path)} nodes):")
207	        IO.puts("")
208	
209	        path
210	        |> Enum.with_index()
211	        |> Enum.each(fn {id, i} ->
212	          a = Map.get(idx, id)
213	          connector = if i == 0, do: "  ", else: "  -> "
214	          IO.puts("#{connector}#{a.id} [#{a.type}] #{trunc(a.claim, 60)}")
215	        end)
216	
217	        IO.puts("")
218	
219	      :no_path ->
220	        IO.puts("No path between #{id1} and #{id2}.")
221	    end
222	  end
223	
224	  defp cmd_history(id) do
225	    {:ok, beliefs} = Store.read()
226	    id = resolve_or_halt(beliefs, id)
227	    idx = Graph.index(beliefs)
228	    target = Map.get(idx, id)
229	    {predecessors, successors} = Graph.history(id, beliefs)
230	    chain = predecessors ++ [target] ++ successors
231	
232	    if length(chain) == 1 do
233	      IO.puts("#{id} has no supersession history (standalone).")
234	    else
235	      IO.puts("")
236	      IO.puts("Supersession chain (#{length(chain)} beliefs):")
237	      IO.puts("")
238	
239	      chain
240	      |> Enum.with_index()
241	      |> Enum.each(fn {a, i} ->
242	        marker = if a.id == id, do: " <-- current", else: ""
243	
244	        status =
245	          case a.status do
246	            "active" -> ""
247	            s -> " [#{s}]"
248	          end
249	
250	        arrow = if i > 0, do: "  -> ", else: "  "
251	        IO.puts("#{arrow}#{a.id}#{status} #{trunc(a.claim, 50)} (#{a.created || "?"})#{marker}")
252	      end)
253	
254	      IO.puts("")
255	    end
256	  end
257	
258	  defp cmd_subjects(args) do
259	    {:ok, beliefs} = Store.read()
260	
261	    results =
262	      case args do
263	        [] ->
264	          IO.puts(:stderr, "Usage: mix bs subjects <ref|--type TYPE>")
265	          System.halt(1)
266	
267	        ["--type", type | _] ->
268	          Graph.by_subject(beliefs, type: type)
269	
270	        [ref | _] ->
271	          if String.contains?(ref, "/") do
272	            Graph.by_subject(beliefs, ref: ref)
273	          else
274	            Graph.by_subject(beliefs, type: ref)
275	          end
276	      end
277	
278	    active = Enum.filter(results, &(&1.status == "active"))
279	
280	    if active == [] do
281	      IO.puts("No active beliefs found for that subject.")
282	    else
283	      Formatter.table(active, length(beliefs)) |> Enum.each(&IO.puts/1)
284	    end
285	  end
286	
287	  defp cmd_stats do
288	    {:ok, beliefs} = Store.read()
289	    s = Graph.stats(beliefs)
290	
291	    IO.puts("")
292	    IO.puts("Belief DAG Statistics")
293	    IO.puts("=====================")
294	    IO.puts("")
295	    IO.puts("Total: #{s.total}")
296	    IO.puts("")
297	
298	    IO.puts("By type:")
299	    Enum.each(s.by_type, fn {k, v} -> IO.puts("  #{k}: #{v}") end)
300	    IO.puts("")
301	
302	    IO.puts("By status:")
303	    Enum.each(s.by_status, fn {k, v} -> IO.puts("  #{k}: #{v}") end)
304	    IO.puts("")
305	
306	    IO.puts("Stale: #{s.stale_count}")
307	    IO.puts("Unlinked implications: #{s.unlinked_implications}")
308	    IO.puts("")
309	
310	    if s.artifact_schemes != %{} do
311	      IO.puts("Artifact schemes:")
312	
313	      s.artifact_schemes
314	      |> Enum.sort_by(fn {_, v} -> -v end)
315	      |> Enum.each(fn {k, v} -> IO.puts("  #{k}: #{v}") end)
316	
317	      IO.puts("")
318	    end
319	
320	    if s.dep_depths != [] do
321	      max_depth = List.last(s.dep_depths)
322	      mean_depth = Enum.sum(s.dep_depths) / max(length(s.dep_depths), 1)
323	      IO.puts("Dependency depth:")
324	      IO.puts("  max: #{max_depth}")
325	      IO.puts("  mean: #{:erlang.float_to_binary(mean_depth * 1.0, decimals: 1)}")
326	      IO.puts("")
327	    end
328	
329	    if s.most_depended != [] do
330	      IO.puts("Most depended-on:")
331	
332	      Enum.each(s.most_depended, fn {id, count} ->
333	        IO.puts("  #{id}: #{count} dependents")
334	      end)
335	
336	      IO.puts("")
337	    end
338	  end
339	
340	  defp cmd_help do
341	    IO.puts("""
342	
343	    bs - belief shell (deterministic DAG queries)
344	
345	    COMMANDS
346	      list [filters]       List beliefs matching filters
347	      show <id>            Full detail on a single belief
348	      tree <id>            Dependency tree visualization
349	      deps <id>            Direct dependencies (--deep for full tree)
350	      dependents <id>      Reverse dependency lookup (--deep for transitive)
351	      stale                Find beliefs with bad deps (--cascade for transitive)
352	      path <id1> <id2>     Find connection between two beliefs
353	      history <id>         Supersession chain
354	      subjects <ref|type>  Find beliefs by subject
355	      stats                Graph-level statistics
356	
357	    FILTERS (for list)
358	      primitive|compound|implication   Filter by structural type
359	      active|superseded|retracted|all  Filter by status (default: active)
360	      contracts                        Contract-grade implications only
361	      unlinked                         Implications with no materialized items
362	      tag:<tag> / kind:<kind> / domain:<domain> / subject_type:<type>
363	
364	    FLAGS
365	      -v / --verbose       Show full detail in list view
366	      --deep               Recurse through full chain (deps, dependents)
367	      --cascade            Include transitively stale (stale)
368	
369	    IDS
370	      Bare (c029) or namespaced (cb:c029). A bare id resolves to its
371	      namespaced form when exactly one belief matches.
372	
373	    COLLECTION
374	      --beliefs PATH       Query an alternate graph (CB_BELIEFS env var too),
375	                           e.g. ../belief-collections/library/beliefs.json
376	    """)
377	  end
378	
379	  # --- collection override ---
380	
381	  # `--beliefs PATH` points the shell at an alternate collection (e.g. a
382	  # belief-collection) by setting the path the store reads, for this task run
383	  # only. Returns args with the flag and its value removed.
384	  defp apply_beliefs_override(args) do
385	    case Enum.split_while(args, &(&1 != "--beliefs")) do
386	      {before, ["--beliefs", path | rest]} ->
387	        Application.put_env(:cb, :beliefs_path, path)
388	        before ++ rest
389	
390	      {_before, ["--beliefs"]} ->
391	        IO.puts(:stderr, "--beliefs requires a path argument")
392	        System.halt(1)
393	
394	      _ ->
395	        args
396	    end
397	  end
398	
399	  # --- id resolution ---
400	
401	  defp resolve_or_halt(beliefs, id) do
402	    case Graph.resolve_id(beliefs, id) do
403	      {:ok, canonical} ->
404	        canonical
405	
406	      {:error, :not_found} ->
407	        IO.puts(:stderr, "No belief with id: #{id}")
408	        System.halt(1)
409	
410	      {:error, {:ambiguous, ids}} ->
411	        IO.puts(:stderr, "Ambiguous id '#{id}' matches: #{Enum.join(ids, ", ")}")
412	        IO.puts(:stderr, "Qualify it with a namespace, e.g. #{hd(ids)}.")
413	        System.halt(1)
414	    end
415	  end
416	
417	  # --- Flag extraction ---
418	
419	  defp extract_flags(args) do
420	    Enum.reduce(args, {[], []}, fn
421	      arg, {flags, rest} when arg in ~w(-v --verbose) ->
422	        {[{:verbose, true} | flags], rest}
423	
424	      "--deep", {flags, rest} ->
425	        {[{:deep, true} | flags], rest}
426	
427	      "--cascade", {flags, rest} ->
428	        {[{:cascade, true} | flags], rest}
429	
430	      arg, {flags, rest} ->
431	        {flags, rest ++ [arg]}
432	    end)
433	  end
434	
435	  defp flag_args(flags) do
436	    Enum.flat_map(flags, fn
437	      {:verbose, true} -> ["-v"]
438	      _ -> []
439	    end)
440	  end
441	end
442

defmodule Mix.Tasks.Bs do
  @moduledoc """
  Belief shell - deterministic query interface to the composable beliefs DAG.

  Pure graph traversal - no LLM reasoning, same input always produces
  the same output.

      list [filters]       List beliefs matching filters
      show <id>            Full detail on a single belief
      tree <id>            Dependency tree visualization
      deps <id>            Direct dependencies (--deep for full tree)
      dependents <id>      Reverse dependency lookup (--deep for transitive)
      stale                Find beliefs with superseded/retracted deps (--cascade)
      path <id1> <id2>     Find connection between two beliefs
      history <id>         Supersession chain
      subjects <ref|type>  Find beliefs by subject
      stats                Graph-level statistics

  ## Ids

  Ids may be given bare (`c029`) or namespaced (`cb:c029`). A bare local id
  resolves to its namespaced form when exactly one belief matches; if more
  than one namespace carries that local id, qualify it explicitly. `mix bs
  <id>` with no verb is shorthand for `show`.

  ## Collections

  `--beliefs PATH` points the shell at an alternate belief graph for one
  command (the `CB_BELIEFS` env var does the same persistently) - used to
  explore other belief collections without touching the main graph.

  ## Filters (for list)

      primitive|compound|implication    Filter by structural type
      active|superseded|retracted|all   Filter by status (default: active)
      contracts                         Contract-grade implications only
      unlinked                          Implications with no materialized items
      tag:<tag> / --tag <tag>           Filter by tag
      kind:<kind>                       Filter by semantic kind
      domain:<domain>                   Filter by domain
      subject_type:<type>              Filter by subject type
      <namespace>/<ref>                Filter by subject ref
      -v / --verbose                    Show full detail
  """
  @shortdoc "Belief shell - query and traverse the composable beliefs DAG"

  use Mix.Task

  import CB.Display

  alias CB.Belief.{Filter, Formatter, Graph, Store}

  @impl Mix.Task
  def run(args) do
    args = apply_beliefs_override(args)
    {flags, positional} = extract_flags(args)

    case positional do
      ["list" | rest] -> cmd_list(rest ++ flag_args(flags))
      ["show", id | _] -> cmd_show(id)
      ["tree", id | _] -> cmd_tree(id)
      ["deps", id | _] -> cmd_deps(id, flags)
      ["dependents", id | _] -> cmd_dependents(id, flags)
      ["stale" | _] -> cmd_stale(flags)
      ["path", id1, id2 | _] -> cmd_path(id1, id2)
      ["history", id | _] -> cmd_history(id)
      ["subjects" | rest] -> cmd_subjects(rest)
      ["stats" | _] -> cmd_stats()
      ["help" | _] -> cmd_help()
      [] -> cmd_help()

      [cmd | _] ->
        if Regex.match?(~r/^([a-z][a-z0-9-]*:)?[ac]\d+$/, cmd) do
          cmd_show(cmd)
        else
          IO.puts(:stderr, "Unknown command: #{cmd}")
          IO.puts(:stderr, "Run `mix bs help` for usage.")
          System.halt(1)
        end
    end
  end

  # --- Commands ---

  defp cmd_list(args) do
    {filters, opts} = Filter.parse_args(args)

    for arg <- Keyword.get_values(opts, :unknown) do
      IO.puts(:stderr, "Unknown filter: #{arg}")
      System.halt(1)
    end

    {:ok, beliefs} = Store.read()
    total = length(beliefs)

    filtered = beliefs |> Filter.apply_filters(filters) |> Filter.sort()

    lines =
      if Keyword.get(opts, :verbose) do
        Enum.flat_map(filtered, &Formatter.detail/1) ++
          ["#{length(filtered)} beliefs (of #{total} total)"]
      else
        Formatter.table(filtered, total)
      end

    Enum.each(lines, &IO.puts/1)
  end

  defp cmd_show(id) do
    {:ok, beliefs} = Store.read()
    id = resolve_or_halt(beliefs, id)
    belief = Enum.find(beliefs, &(&1.id == id))
    Formatter.detail(belief) |> Enum.each(&IO.puts/1)
  end

  defp cmd_tree(id) do
    {:ok, beliefs} = Store.read()
    id = resolve_or_halt(beliefs, id)
    belief = Enum.find(beliefs, &(&1.id == id))
    Formatter.tree(belief, beliefs) |> Enum.each(&IO.puts/1)
  end

  defp cmd_deps(id, flags) do
    {:ok, beliefs} = Store.read()
    id = resolve_or_halt(beliefs, id)
    idx = Graph.index(beliefs)
    belief = Map.get(idx, id)

    if Keyword.get(flags, :deep) do
      cmd_tree(id)
    else
      resolved = Graph.resolve_deps(belief, idx)

      if resolved == [] do
        IO.puts("#{id} has no dependencies (primitive).")
      else
        Formatter.table(resolved, length(beliefs)) |> Enum.each(&IO.puts/1)
      end
    end
  end

  defp cmd_dependents(id, flags) do
    {:ok, beliefs} = Store.read()
    id = resolve_or_halt(beliefs, id)
    deep = Keyword.get(flags, :deep, false)
    results = Graph.dependents(id, beliefs, deep: deep)

    if results == [] do
      IO.puts("Nothing depends on #{id}.")
    else
      label = if deep, do: "deep dependents", else: "dependents"
      IO.puts("")
      IO.puts("#{length(results)} #{label} of #{id}:")
      IO.puts("")
      Formatter.table(results, length(beliefs)) |> Enum.each(&IO.puts/1)
    end
  end

  defp cmd_stale(flags) do
    {:ok, beliefs} = Store.read()
    cascade = Keyword.get(flags, :cascade, false)
    results = Graph.stale(beliefs, cascade: cascade)

    if results == [] do
      IO.puts("No stale beliefs found.")
    else
      idx = Graph.index(beliefs)
      label = if cascade, do: " (with cascade)", else: ""
      IO.puts("")
      IO.puts("Stale beliefs#{label}:")
      IO.puts("")

      Enum.each(results, fn {a, bad_deps} ->
        IO.puts("  #{a.id} #{a.claim}")

        Enum.each(bad_deps, fn dep_id ->
          dep = Map.get(idx, dep_id)

          reason =
            cond do
              dep == nil -> "missing"
              dep.superseded_by -> "superseded by #{dep.superseded_by}"
              dep.status == "retracted" -> "retracted"
              true -> dep.status
            end

          IO.puts("    #{dep_id}: #{reason}")
        end)

        IO.puts("")
      end)

      IO.puts("#{length(results)} stale belief(s)")
    end
  end

  defp cmd_path(id1, id2) do
    {:ok, beliefs} = Store.read()
    id1 = resolve_or_halt(beliefs, id1)
    id2 = resolve_or_halt(beliefs, id2)
    idx = Graph.index(beliefs)

    case Graph.path(id1, id2, idx, beliefs) do
      {:ok, path} ->
        IO.puts("")
        IO.puts("Path from #{id1} to #{id2} (#{length(path)} nodes):")
        IO.puts("")

        path
        |> Enum.with_index()
        |> Enum.each(fn {id, i} ->
          a = Map.get(idx, id)
          connector = if i == 0, do: "  ", else: "  -> "
          IO.puts("#{connector}#{a.id} [#{a.type}] #{trunc(a.claim, 60)}")
        end)

        IO.puts("")

      :no_path ->
        IO.puts("No path between #{id1} and #{id2}.")
    end
  end

  defp cmd_history(id) do
    {:ok, beliefs} = Store.read()
    id = resolve_or_halt(beliefs, id)
    idx = Graph.index(beliefs)
    target = Map.get(idx, id)
    {predecessors, successors} = Graph.history(id, beliefs)
    chain = predecessors ++ [target] ++ successors

    if length(chain) == 1 do
      IO.puts("#{id} has no supersession history (standalone).")
    else
      IO.puts("")
      IO.puts("Supersession chain (#{length(chain)} beliefs):")
      IO.puts("")

      chain
      |> Enum.with_index()
      |> Enum.each(fn {a, i} ->
        marker = if a.id == id, do: " <-- current", else: ""

        status =
          case a.status do
            "active" -> ""
            s -> " [#{s}]"
          end

        arrow = if i > 0, do: "  -> ", else: "  "
        IO.puts("#{arrow}#{a.id}#{status} #{trunc(a.claim, 50)} (#{a.created || "?"})#{marker}")
      end)

      IO.puts("")
    end
  end

  defp cmd_subjects(args) do
    {:ok, beliefs} = Store.read()

    results =
      case args do
        [] ->
          IO.puts(:stderr, "Usage: mix bs subjects <ref|--type TYPE>")
          System.halt(1)

        ["--type", type | _] ->
          Graph.by_subject(beliefs, type: type)

        [ref | _] ->
          if String.contains?(ref, "/") do
            Graph.by_subject(beliefs, ref: ref)
          else
            Graph.by_subject(beliefs, type: ref)
          end
      end

    active = Enum.filter(results, &(&1.status == "active"))

    if active == [] do
      IO.puts("No active beliefs found for that subject.")
    else
      Formatter.table(active, length(beliefs)) |> Enum.each(&IO.puts/1)
    end
  end

  defp cmd_stats do
    {:ok, beliefs} = Store.read()
    s = Graph.stats(beliefs)

    IO.puts("")
    IO.puts("Belief DAG Statistics")
    IO.puts("=====================")
    IO.puts("")
    IO.puts("Total: #{s.total}")
    IO.puts("")

    IO.puts("By type:")
    Enum.each(s.by_type, fn {k, v} -> IO.puts("  #{k}: #{v}") end)
    IO.puts("")

    IO.puts("By status:")
    Enum.each(s.by_status, fn {k, v} -> IO.puts("  #{k}: #{v}") end)
    IO.puts("")

    IO.puts("Stale: #{s.stale_count}")
    IO.puts("Unlinked implications: #{s.unlinked_implications}")
    IO.puts("")

    if s.artifact_schemes != %{} do
      IO.puts("Artifact schemes:")

      s.artifact_schemes
      |> Enum.sort_by(fn {_, v} -> -v end)
      |> Enum.each(fn {k, v} -> IO.puts("  #{k}: #{v}") end)

      IO.puts("")
    end

    if s.dep_depths != [] do
      max_depth = List.last(s.dep_depths)
      mean_depth = Enum.sum(s.dep_depths) / max(length(s.dep_depths), 1)
      IO.puts("Dependency depth:")
      IO.puts("  max: #{max_depth}")
      IO.puts("  mean: #{:erlang.float_to_binary(mean_depth * 1.0, decimals: 1)}")
      IO.puts("")
    end

    if s.most_depended != [] do
      IO.puts("Most depended-on:")

      Enum.each(s.most_depended, fn {id, count} ->
        IO.puts("  #{id}: #{count} dependents")
      end)

      IO.puts("")
    end
  end

  defp cmd_help do
    IO.puts("""

    bs - belief shell (deterministic DAG queries)

    COMMANDS
      list [filters]       List beliefs matching filters
      show <id>            Full detail on a single belief
      tree <id>            Dependency tree visualization
      deps <id>            Direct dependencies (--deep for full tree)
      dependents <id>      Reverse dependency lookup (--deep for transitive)
      stale                Find beliefs with bad deps (--cascade for transitive)
      path <id1> <id2>     Find connection between two beliefs
      history <id>         Supersession chain
      subjects <ref|type>  Find beliefs by subject
      stats                Graph-level statistics

    FILTERS (for list)
      primitive|compound|implication   Filter by structural type
      active|superseded|retracted|all  Filter by status (default: active)
      contracts                        Contract-grade implications only
      unlinked                         Implications with no materialized items
      tag:<tag> / kind:<kind> / domain:<domain> / subject_type:<type>

    FLAGS
      -v / --verbose       Show full detail in list view
      --deep               Recurse through full chain (deps, dependents)
      --cascade            Include transitively stale (stale)

    IDS
      Bare (c029) or namespaced (cb:c029). A bare id resolves to its
      namespaced form when exactly one belief matches.

    COLLECTION
      --beliefs PATH       Query an alternate graph (CB_BELIEFS env var too),
                           e.g. ../belief-collections/library/beliefs.json
    """)
  end

  # --- collection override ---

  # `--beliefs PATH` points the shell at an alternate collection (e.g. a
  # belief-collection) by setting the path the store reads, for this task run
  # only. Returns args with the flag and its value removed.
  defp apply_beliefs_override(args) do
    case Enum.split_while(args, &(&1 != "--beliefs")) do
      {before, ["--beliefs", path | rest]} ->
        Application.put_env(:cb, :beliefs_path, path)
        before ++ rest

      {_before, ["--beliefs"]} ->
        IO.puts(:stderr, "--beliefs requires a path argument")
        System.halt(1)

      _ ->
        args
    end
  end

  # --- id resolution ---

  defp resolve_or_halt(beliefs, id) do
    case Graph.resolve_id(beliefs, id) do
      {:ok, canonical} ->
        canonical

      {:error, :not_found} ->
        IO.puts(:stderr, "No belief with id: #{id}")
        System.halt(1)

      {:error, {:ambiguous, ids}} ->
        IO.puts(:stderr, "Ambiguous id '#{id}' matches: #{Enum.join(ids, ", ")}")
        IO.puts(:stderr, "Qualify it with a namespace, e.g. #{hd(ids)}.")
        System.halt(1)
    end
  end

  # --- Flag extraction ---

  defp extract_flags(args) do
    Enum.reduce(args, {[], []}, fn
      arg, {flags, rest} when arg in ~w(-v --verbose) ->
        {[{:verbose, true} | flags], rest}

      "--deep", {flags, rest} ->
        {[{:deep, true} | flags], rest}

      "--cascade", {flags, rest} ->
        {[{:cascade, true} | flags], rest}

      arg, {flags, rest} ->
        {flags, rest ++ [arg]}
    end)
  end

  defp flag_args(flags) do
    Enum.flat_map(flags, fn
      {:verbose, true} -> ["-v"]
      _ -> []
    end)
  end
end

defmodule CB.Belief.Filter do
  @moduledoc """
  Parses CLI filter arguments and applies them to belief lists.
  """

  def parse_args(args) do
    {flags, positional} = extract_flags(args)

    {filters, opts} =
      Enum.reduce(positional, {[], []}, fn arg, {filters, opts} ->
        cond do
          # Structural type filter
          arg in ~w(primitive compound implication) ->
            {[&(&1.type == arg) | filters], opts}

          # Status filter
          arg in ~w(active superseded retracted retired) ->
            {[&(&1.status == arg) | filters], [{:status_override, true} | opts]}

          arg == "all" ->
            {filters, [{:status_override, true} | opts]}

          arg == "stale" ->
            {filters, [{:stale, true} | opts]}

          arg == "contracts" ->
            {[&CB.Belief.contract?/1 | filters], opts}

          arg == "unlinked" ->
            {[&(&1.type == "implication" and &1.materialized == nil) | filters], opts}

          # Tag filter: --tag <tag> or tag:<tag>
          String.starts_with?(arg, "tag:") ->
            tag = String.replace_prefix(arg, "tag:", "")
            {[&(tag in (&1.tags || [])) | filters], opts}

          # Kind filter: kind:<kind>
          String.starts_with?(arg, "kind:") ->
            k = String.replace_prefix(arg, "kind:", "")
            {[&(&1.kind == k) | filters], opts}

          # Domain filter: domain:<domain>
          String.starts_with?(arg, "domain:") ->
            d = String.replace_prefix(arg, "domain:", "")
            {[&(&1.domain == d) | filters], opts}

          String.starts_with?(arg, "subject_type:") ->
            st = String.replace_prefix(arg, "subject_type:", "")
            {[&(Enum.any?(&1.subjects || [], fn s -> s["type"] == st end)) | filters], opts}

          String.contains?(arg, "/") ->
            {[&(Enum.any?(&1.subjects || [], fn s -> s["ref"] == arg end)) | filters], opts}

          true ->
            {filters, [{:unknown, arg} | opts]}
        end
      end)

    # Handle --tag flag from extract_flags
    filters =
      Enum.reduce(Keyword.get_values(flags, :tag), filters, fn tag, acc ->
        [&(tag in (&1.tags || [])) | acc]
      end)

    flags = Keyword.delete(flags, :tag)

    filters =
      if Keyword.has_key?(opts, :status_override) do
        filters
      else
        [&(&1.status == "active") | filters]
      end

    merged_opts = Keyword.merge(flags, opts)
    {Enum.reverse(filters), merged_opts}
  end

  def apply_filters(beliefs, filters) do
    Enum.filter(beliefs, fn a ->
      Enum.all?(filters, fn f -> f.(a) end)
    end)
  end

  def sort(beliefs) do
    type_order = %{"primitive" => 0, "compound" => 1, "implication" => 2}

    Enum.sort_by(beliefs, fn a ->
      {Map.get(type_order, a.type, 2), a.id}
    end)
  end

  defp extract_flags(args) do
    extract_flags(args, [], [])
  end

  defp extract_flags([], flags, rest), do: {flags, rest}

  defp extract_flags([arg | tail], flags, rest) when arg in ~w(-v --verbose) do
    extract_flags(tail, [{:verbose, true} | flags], rest)
  end

  defp extract_flags(["--tag", tag | tail], flags, rest) do
    extract_flags(tail, [{:tag, tag} | flags], rest)
  end

  defp extract_flags([arg | tail], flags, rest) do
    extract_flags(tail, flags, rest ++ [arg])
  end
end

defmodule CB.OutputTarget do
  @moduledoc """
  Shared logic for compiling output-target contracts into markdown files.

  An output-target contract is a `type: "implication"` node with
  `kind: "output-target"` that declares:
  - `output_path` (in rules): where to write the file
  - `header_comment` (in rules, optional): top-of-file comment
  - `paths` (in rules, optional): frontmatter for scoped rule files
  - `render_sections` (in rules): [{"title": "...", "beliefs": [ids]}]

  The compiler reads the contract, dereferences each belief ID in
  render_sections to its claim field, and emits the file. Every
  line in the output traces to exactly one belief claim.
  """

  alias CB.Belief
  alias CB.Belief.Store, as: BeliefStore

  @doc """
  Find all active output-target contracts matching an optional filter tag.

  Pass `tag: "output:claude-md"` for a CLAUDE.md manifest, or
  `tag: "output:rule"` for rule file manifests.
  """
  def find_targets(opts \\ []) do
    filter_tag = Keyword.get(opts, :tag)

    with {:ok, all} <- BeliefStore.read() do
      targets =
        all
        |> Enum.filter(&output_target?/1)
        |> filter_by_tag(filter_tag)

      {:ok, targets, all}
    end
  end

  @doc """
  Compile one output-target contract into its rendered markdown string.

  Takes the contract struct plus the full list of beliefs (used to
  dereference IDs in render_sections). Returns `{:ok, path, content}`
  or `{:error, reason}`.
  """
  def compile(target, all_beliefs) do
    by_id = Map.new(all_beliefs, &{&1.id, &1})
    rules = extract_rules_map(target)

    with {:ok, output_path} <- fetch_rule(rules, "output_path"),
         {:ok, sections} <- fetch_rule(rules, "render_sections") do
      header_comment = Map.get(rules, "header_comment", "")
      paths = Map.get(rules, "paths", nil)

      content =
        build_content(
          header_comment: header_comment,
          paths: paths,
          sections: sections,
          by_id: by_id,
          target_id: target.id
        )

      {:ok, output_path, content}
    end
  end

  # --- Private ---

  defp output_target?(%Belief{status: "active", kind: "output-target"}), do: true
  defp output_target?(_), do: false

  defp filter_by_tag(targets, nil), do: targets
  defp filter_by_tag(targets, tag), do: Enum.filter(targets, &(tag in (&1.tags || [])))

  # Rules on a contract is a list of single-key maps; flatten into one.
  defp extract_rules_map(%{rules: rules}) when is_list(rules) do
    Enum.reduce(rules, %{}, fn
      rule, acc when is_map(rule) -> Map.merge(acc, rule)
      _, acc -> acc
    end)
  end

  defp extract_rules_map(_), do: %{}

  defp fetch_rule(rules, key) do
    case Map.fetch(rules, key) do
      {:ok, value} -> {:ok, value}
      :error -> {:error, {:missing_rule, key}}
    end
  end

  defp build_content(opts) do
    frontmatter = render_frontmatter(opts[:paths])
    header = render_header(opts[:header_comment])
    body = render_sections(opts[:sections], opts[:by_id], opts[:target_id])

    [frontmatter, header, body]
    |> Enum.reject(&(&1 == "" or is_nil(&1)))
    |> Enum.join("\n")
    |> ensure_trailing_newline()
  end

  defp render_frontmatter(nil), do: ""
  defp render_frontmatter([]), do: ""

  defp render_frontmatter(paths) when is_list(paths) do
    path_lines = Enum.map_join(paths, "\n", &"  - \"#{&1}\"")
    "---\npaths:\n#{path_lines}\n---\n"
  end

  defp render_header(nil), do: ""
  defp render_header(""), do: ""
  defp render_header(comment) when is_binary(comment), do: "#{comment}\n"

  defp render_sections(sections, by_id, target_id) when is_list(sections) do
    sections
    |> Enum.map(&render_section(&1, by_id, target_id))
    |> Enum.join("\n")
  end

  defp render_section(%{"title" => title, "beliefs" => belief_ids}, by_id, _target_id) do
    lines =
      belief_ids
      |> Enum.map(fn id ->
        case Map.get(by_id, id) do
          nil -> "<!-- BELIEF NOT FOUND: #{id} -->"
          belief -> belief.claim || "<!-- NO CLAIM: #{id} -->"
        end
      end)
      |> Enum.join("\n\n")

    "## #{title}\n\n#{lines}\n"
  end

  defp render_section(_bad_section, _by_id, target_id) do
    "<!-- BAD SECTION in #{target_id} -->\n"
  end

  defp ensure_trailing_newline(content) do
    if String.ends_with?(content, "\n"), do: content, else: content <> "\n"
  end

  @doc """
  Validate a target's deps match the union of render_sections' beliefs.
  Returns `:ok` or `{:error, {:deps_mismatch, missing, extra}}`.
  """
  def validate_deps_match_sections(target) do
    rules = extract_rules_map(target)
    sections = Map.get(rules, "render_sections", [])

    section_ids =
      sections
      |> Enum.flat_map(fn
        %{"beliefs" => ids} -> ids
        _ -> []
      end)
      |> MapSet.new()

    dep_ids = MapSet.new(target.deps || [])

    missing = MapSet.difference(section_ids, dep_ids) |> MapSet.to_list()
    extra = MapSet.difference(dep_ids, section_ids) |> MapSet.to_list()

    if missing == [] and extra == [] do
      :ok
    else
      {:error, {:deps_mismatch, missing, extra}}
    end
  end
end

1	defmodule CB.Schema.Verifier do
2	  @moduledoc """
3	  Verify a belief collection against the schema contracts it carries.
4	
5	  The verifier is collection-agnostic. Two kinds of rule are checked:
6	
7	  - **Framework-universal structure** - the three structural types, the
8	    `contract: true` biconditional, the `c`-prefix convention, artifact
9	    format, status linkage. These hold for any well-formed collection and
10	    are checked against `CB.Belief`'s own canon, not against ids.
11	  - **Collection-specific vocabulary** - the closed enums for `kind`,
12	    `domain`, and `artifact-scheme`, plus the status lifecycle. These are
13	    *discovered* from the collection's own contracts by role: an enum is
14	    found by the field it declares (via `CB.Belief.Contract.Enum`), the
15	    status lifecycle by a `status-lifecycle`-tagged state-machine contract
16	    (via `CB.Belief.Contract.StateMachine`). A collection that declares no
17	    enum for a field has that vocabulary check skipped; status falls back
18	    to `CB.Belief.statuses/0`.
19	
20	  This is the same dogfooding the `cb:` graph relies on - its
21	  `cb:c029/c039/c040/c041` are discovered by role like any other contract -
22	  generalized so any belief-collection (or host graph) verifies
23	  against its own schema.
24	
25	  `check/1` is pure: it takes the belief list and returns a list of
26	  `{name, status, detail}` where status is `:ok | :fail | :skip`. The
27	  `mix cb.verify.schema` task is a thin IO wrapper over it.
28	  """
29	
30	  alias CB.Belief
31	  alias CB.Belief.Contract.Enum, as: EnumContract
32	  alias CB.Belief.Contract.StateMachine
33	
34	  @type status :: :ok | :fail | :skip
35	  @type result :: {String.t(), status(), String.t()}
36	
37	  @doc "Run every schema check against `beliefs`. Pure - returns results."
38	  @spec check([Belief.t()]) :: [result()]
39	  def check(beliefs) do
40	    [
41	      check_schema_roles(beliefs),
42	      check_type_enum(beliefs),
43	      check_contract_requires_implication(beliefs),
44	      check_contract_biconditional(beliefs),
45	      check_kind_enum(beliefs),
46	      check_domain_enum(beliefs),
47	      check_artifact_format(beliefs),
48	      check_artifact_scheme_enum(beliefs),
49	      check_no_implication_field(beliefs),
50	      check_action_item_shape(beliefs),
51	      check_compound_implication_have_deps(beliefs),
52	      check_status_enum(beliefs),
53	      check_superseded_linkage(beliefs),
54	      check_retracted_linkage(beliefs),
55	      check_c_prefix_is_contract(beliefs)
56	    ]
57	  end
58	
59	  # --- role discovery (no hardcoded ids) ---
60	
61	  defp active_contracts(beliefs) do
62	    Enum.filter(beliefs, &(&1.status == "active" and &1.contract == true))
63	  end
64	
65	  # The active enum-registry contract that declares `field`, or nil.
66	  defp enum_contract_for(beliefs, field) do
67	    beliefs
68	    |> active_contracts()
69	    |> Enum.filter(&(&1.kind == "enum-registry"))
70	    |> Enum.find(fn c -> field in EnumContract.fields(c) end)
71	  end
72	
73	  # The active state-machine contract governing the belief status lifecycle,
74	  # identified by the `status-lifecycle` tag, or nil. (A collection may carry
75	  # other state machines - e.g. a domain entity's lifecycle - so the role is
76	  # marked by tag rather than inferred from kind alone.)
77	  defp status_lifecycle_contract(beliefs) do
78	    beliefs
79	    |> active_contracts()
80	    |> Enum.filter(&(&1.kind == "state-machine"))
81	    |> Enum.find(fn c -> "status-lifecycle" in (c.tags || []) end)
82	  end
83	
84	  # --- schema roles present ---
85	
86	  defp check_schema_roles(beliefs) do
87	    detail =
88	      "kind=#{role_id(enum_contract_for(beliefs, "kind"))}, " <>
89	        "domain=#{role_id(enum_contract_for(beliefs, "domain"))}, " <>
90	        "artifact-scheme=#{role_id(enum_contract_for(beliefs, "artifact-scheme"))}, " <>
91	        "status-lifecycle=#{role_id(status_lifecycle_contract(beliefs), "framework canon")}"
92	
93	    {"schema roles discovered", :ok, detail}
94	  end
95	
96	  defp role_id(contract, absent \\ "none")
97	  defp role_id(nil, absent), do: absent
98	  defp role_id(%{id: id}, _absent), do: id
99	
100	  # --- type enum (framework-universal) ---
101	
102	  defp check_type_enum(beliefs) do
103	    valid = Belief.types()
104	    bad = beliefs |> Enum.reject(&(&1.type in valid)) |> Enum.map(& &1.id)
105	
106	    if bad == [] do
107	      {"type enum", :ok, "all nodes have type in #{inspect(valid)}"}
108	    else
109	      {"type enum", :fail, "nodes with invalid type: #{inspect(bad)}"}
110	    end
111	  end
112	
113	  # --- contract structural rules (framework-universal) ---
114	
115	  defp check_contract_requires_implication(beliefs) do
116	    violations =
117	      beliefs
118	      |> Enum.filter(&(&1.contract == true))
119	      |> Enum.reject(&(&1.type == "implication"))
120	      |> Enum.map(& &1.id)
121	
122	    if violations == [] do
123	      {"contract requires implication", :ok, "all contract-grade beliefs are implications"}
124	    else
125	      {"contract requires implication", :fail, "contract: true on non-implication: #{inspect(violations)}"}
126	    end
127	  end
128	
129	  defp check_contract_biconditional(beliefs) do
130	    # contract: true iff (rules non-empty OR invariants non-empty)
131	    violations =
132	      beliefs
133	      |> Enum.filter(fn a ->
134	        has_payload =
135	          (is_list(a.rules) and a.rules != []) or (is_list(a.invariants) and a.invariants != [])
136	
137	        has_payload != (a.contract == true)
138	      end)
139	      |> Enum.map(& &1.id)
140	
141	    if violations == [] do
142	      {"contract biconditional", :ok, "contract: true iff rules/invariants non-empty"}
143	    else
144	      {"contract biconditional", :fail, "contract/payload mismatch: #{inspect(violations)}"}
145	    end
146	  end
147	
148	  # --- kind / domain enums (discovered by field) ---
149	
150	  defp check_kind_enum(beliefs), do: check_field_enum(beliefs, "kind", & &1.kind)
151	  defp check_domain_enum(beliefs), do: check_field_enum(beliefs, "domain", & &1.domain)
152	
153	  defp check_field_enum(beliefs, field, getter) do
154	    case enum_contract_for(beliefs, field) do
155	      nil ->
156	        {"#{field} enum", :skip, "no active enum-registry contract declares #{field}"}
157	
158	      contract ->
159	        allowed = MapSet.new(EnumContract.values_for(contract, field))
160	
161	        violations =
162	          beliefs
163	          |> Enum.filter(&(&1.status == "active" and not is_nil(getter.(&1))))
164	          |> Enum.reject(&MapSet.member?(allowed, getter.(&1)))
165	          |> Enum.map(&{&1.id, getter.(&1)})
166	
167	        if violations == [] do
168	          {"#{field} enum", :ok,
169	           "all active beliefs use #{field} values declared in #{contract.id} (#{MapSet.size(allowed)} values)"}
170	        else
171	          {"#{field} enum", :fail, "#{field} values outside #{contract.id} enum: #{inspect(violations)}"}
172	        end
173	    end
174	  end
175	
176	  # --- artifact format and scheme ---
177	
178	  defp check_artifact_format(beliefs) do
179	    # artifact is null OR matches /^[a-z][a-z0-9_-]*:.+/
180	    regex = ~r/^[a-z][a-z0-9_-]*:.+/
181	
182	    violations =
183	      beliefs
184	      |> Enum.filter(&(is_binary(&1.artifact) and &1.artifact != ""))
185	      |> Enum.reject(&Regex.match?(regex, &1.artifact))
186	      |> Enum.map(&{&1.id, &1.artifact})
187	
188	    if violations == [] do
189	      {"artifact format", :ok, "all artifacts match scheme:id"}
190	    else
191	      {"artifact format", :fail, "artifacts violating scheme:id form: #{inspect(violations)}"}
192	    end
193	  end
194	
195	  defp check_artifact_scheme_enum(beliefs) do
196	    case enum_contract_for(beliefs, "artifact-scheme") do
197	      nil ->
198	        {"artifact-scheme enum", :skip, "no active enum-registry contract declares artifact-scheme"}
199	
200	      contract ->
201	        allowed = MapSet.new(EnumContract.values_for(contract, "artifact-scheme"))
202	
203	        violations =
204	          beliefs
205	          |> Enum.filter(&(&1.status == "active" and is_binary(&1.artifact)))
206	          |> Enum.map(fn a -> {a.id, scheme(a.artifact)} end)
207	          |> Enum.reject(fn {_, s} -> MapSet.member?(allowed, s) end)
208	
209	        if violations == [] do
210	          {"artifact-scheme enum", :ok,
211	           "all artifact schemes declared in #{contract.id} (#{MapSet.size(allowed)} schemes)"}
212	        else
213	          {"artifact-scheme enum", :fail,
214	           "artifact schemes outside #{contract.id} enum: #{inspect(violations)}"}
215	        end
216	    end
217	  end
218	
219	  # --- implication field absent (framework-universal) ---
220	
221	  defp check_no_implication_field(beliefs) do
222	    violations =
223	      beliefs
224	      |> Enum.filter(fn a -> a._keys && MapSet.member?(a._keys, "implication") end)
225	      |> Enum.map(& &1.id)
226	
227	    if violations == [] do
228	      {"no implication field", :ok, "no belief carries the deleted implication field"}
229	    else
230	      {"no implication field", :fail, "beliefs still carrying implication: #{inspect(violations)}"}
231	    end
232	  end
233	
234	  # --- action-item shape (framework-universal) ---
235	
236	  defp check_action_item_shape(beliefs) do
237	    violations =
238	      beliefs
239	      |> Enum.filter(&(&1.kind == "action-item"))
240	      |> Enum.filter(fn a ->
241	        a.type != "implication" or a.contract == true or
242	          (is_list(a.rules) and a.rules != []) or
243	          (is_list(a.invariants) and a.invariants != [])
244	      end)
245	      |> Enum.map(& &1.id)
246	
247	    if violations == [] do
248	      {"action-item shape", :ok, "all action-items are non-contract implications with empty rules/invariants"}
249	    else
250	      {"action-item shape", :fail, "action-items violating shape: #{inspect(violations)}"}
251	    end
252	  end
253	
254	  # --- compounds and derived implications have deps ---
255	
256	  defp check_compound_implication_have_deps(beliefs) do
257	    # Compounds must have deps. Implications must have deps UNLESS they are
258	    # contract-grade - contracts may be declared from policy without composing.
259	    violations =
260	      beliefs
261	      |> Enum.filter(&(&1.status == "active"))
262	      |> Enum.filter(fn a ->
263	        cond do
264	          a.type == "compound" -> not (is_list(a.deps) and a.deps != [])
265	          a.type == "implication" -> a.contract != true and not (is_list(a.deps) and a.deps != [])
266	          true -> false
267	        end
268	      end)
269	      |> Enum.map(& &1.id)
270	
271	    if violations == [] do
272	      {"compound/implication deps", :ok, "all active compounds and non-contract implications have non-empty deps"}
273	    else
274	      {"compound/implication deps", :fail, "nodes without deps: #{inspect(violations)}"}
275	    end
276	  end
277	
278	  # --- status lifecycle (discovered SM, or framework canon) ---
279	
280	  defp check_status_enum(beliefs) do
281	    {source, allowed} =
282	      case status_lifecycle_contract(beliefs) do
283	        nil ->
284	          {"framework canon", Belief.statuses()}
285	
286	        c ->
287	          states =
288	            c
289	            |> StateMachine.edges()
290	            |> Enum.flat_map(fn e -> [e.from, e.to] end)
291	            |> Enum.reject(&is_nil/1)
292	            |> Enum.uniq()
293	
294	          {c.id, states}
295	      end
296	
297	    if allowed == [] do
298	      {"status enum", :skip, "status lifecycle contract has no parseable edges"}
299	    else
300	      allowed_set = MapSet.new(allowed)
301	
302	      violations =
303	        beliefs
304	        |> Enum.reject(&MapSet.member?(allowed_set, &1.status))
305	        |> Enum.map(&{&1.id, &1.status})
306	
307	      if violations == [] do
308	        {"status enum", :ok, "all nodes have status in #{inspect(allowed)} (#{source})"}
309	      else
310	        {"status enum", :fail, "invalid status: #{inspect(violations)}"}
311	      end
312	    end
313	  end
314	
315	  defp check_superseded_linkage(beliefs) do
316	    violations =
317	      beliefs
318	      |> Enum.filter(&(&1.status == "superseded"))
319	      |> Enum.reject(&(is_binary(&1.superseded_by) and &1.superseded_by != ""))
320	      |> Enum.map(& &1.id)
321	
322	    if violations == [] do
323	      {"superseded linkage", :ok, "all superseded nodes link to successor"}
324	    else
325	      {"superseded linkage", :fail, "superseded without link: #{inspect(violations)}"}
326	    end
327	  end
328	
329	  defp check_retracted_linkage(beliefs) do
330	    violations =
331	      beliefs
332	      |> Enum.filter(&(&1.status == "retracted"))
333	      |> Enum.reject(fn a ->
334	        is_binary(a.retracted_on) and a.retracted_on != "" and
335	          is_binary(a.retracted_reason) and a.retracted_reason != ""
336	      end)
337	      |> Enum.map(& &1.id)
338	
339	    if violations == [] do
340	      {"retracted linkage", :ok, "all retracted nodes have date and reason"}
341	    else
342	      {"retracted linkage", :fail, "retracted without metadata: #{inspect(violations)}"}
343	    end
344	  end
345	
346	  # --- c-prefix identity (framework-universal) ---
347	
348	  defp check_c_prefix_is_contract(beliefs) do
349	    # The prefix lives on the local id, so test the segment after the
350	    # namespace (`cb:c038` -> `c038`) rather than the raw id.
351	    mismatches =
352	      beliefs
353	      |> Enum.filter(&String.starts_with?(local_id(&1.id), "c"))
354	      |> Enum.reject(&(&1.contract == true))
355	      |> Enum.map(& &1.id)
356	
357	    if mismatches == [] do
358	      {"c-prefix is contract-grade", :ok, "all c-prefix IDs carry contract: true"}
359	    else
360	      {"c-prefix is contract-grade", :fail, "c-prefix IDs without contract: true: #{inspect(mismatches)}"}
361	    end
362	  end
363	
364	  # --- helpers ---
365	
366	  defp scheme(uri) when is_binary(uri) do
367	    case String.split(uri, ":", parts: 2) do
368	      [s, _] -> s
369	      _ -> ""
370	    end
371	  end
372	
373	  defp local_id(id) when is_binary(id), do: id |> String.split(":") |> List.last()
374	  defp local_id(id), do: id
375	end
376
